chosen_action.title: Tighten the first-run conversion path on README / START_HERE / docs index

why_this_action.summary: External distribution lanes are either blocked, overlapping, or already inside live measurement windows, while the current audited bottleneck is conversion from interest to primary-repo free use. Tightening the first-run path on owned repo surfaces is the cleanest high-leverage action that can ship right now.

what_changed: README route now pushes evaluators through a tighter first-run sequence: START_HERE -> first-task guide -> example first task -> docs switchboard.
START_HERE now includes a copy-paste PROMPT.md contract and a tomorrow-morning merge-or-not review card on the same page.
docs/README now promotes the example first task as an early proof asset instead of burying it behind broader docs browsing.

docs_review_note.what_changed: Reordered the top-level repo path around one honest first run and added the missing copy-paste spec plus morning-after review gate to START_HERE.
docs_review_note.why_here: These belong on README / START_HERE / docs index because they are first-touch conversion surfaces, not deep-reference material.
docs_review_note.what_was_pruned_or_merged: Did not add a new page; instead reused the existing first-task and example assets to reduce routing friction and avoid duplicating guidance.
docs_review_note.why_top_level_is_better: A qualified evaluator can now move from repo landing to task choice to prompt shape to review standard with fewer decisions and without hunting through the docs stack.

checks: python3 inline sanity check asserting Codeberg references remain present in all edited files

expected_outcome: More evaluators reach a concrete first run faster, with a clearer prompt shape and merge-or-not standard that should improve qualified inspection and eventual Codeberg adoption.

measurement_window.success_metric: Evidence that this tighter first-run path is being reused by follow-on marketing assets or coincides with improved Codeberg adoption / issue quality.
measurement_window.kill_condition: If Codeberg adoption and conversion signals stay flat after the next review window, treat this surface change as insufficient and move to a different executable demand-capture or proof lane instead of rewording the same surfaces again.
